extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primoz won mountaintop sprint by a bike length with 10 bonus seconds, converting 6-second deficit into 4-second overall lead
tactical_patterns: Caruso extended 23-second bridge attempt for teammates Potrago/Lander before Mass counterattacked, suggesting Bahrain team-first breakaway strategy.
Enric Mass initiated decisive selection despite struggling on windy descents earlier, revealing he peaks late-stage despite mid-race weakness.
Jumbo-Visma used Kelderman to close gaps before Roglic sprinted, indicating dual-leader strategy with clear sprint hierarchy established.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: won mountaintop sprint against strong field, precisely timed final effort to maximize bonus seconds for GC reversal
rider_signals.MASS Enric: positive: launched race-defining attack after appearing weak on descents, showing compartmentalized strength in crosswinds vs climbing
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: neutral: extended 23-second solo bridge suggesting domestique willing to burn matches for team positioning rather than conserve
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: closed gaps for Roglic rather than protecting own GC position 12 seconds down, confirming team hierarchy
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: negative: absent from finale after previous day's crash, suggesting lingering impact beyond stage of incident
